raw_response: arm extended, chest height, strong, forward, upward.
thinking:
Okay, let's break this down. The user wants me to describe the action of hurling a palm-sized object as a right-handed person would, considering typical native English speaker understanding. The key is to figure out each dimension based on common usage.

First, FORCE: Hurling usually implies a strong action, more than just tossing lightly. So probably "strong" force.

ARM state: When you hurl something, you usually start with your arm extended to get maximum distance. So "arm extended" makes sense.

HAND height: If the arm is extended, the hand would be at chest height when starting. Because if you're reaching forward to throw, your hand would be around chest level initially.

VD (Vertical direction): Hurling involves throwing the object upwards to get it to go further. So primary movement is upward.

HD (Horizontal direction): The main horizontal movement is forward, towards the target.

Putting it all together: The hand starts at chest height, arm extended, applies strong force, moves upward and forward. So the sentence would combine these elements using the given options.
